raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.5,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The draft is built around a gap statement and a strong novelty positioning claim ('first framework to enforce typed inter-agent communication'); improving the draft plausibly requires sharpening both even without an explicit instruction to do so.","R1.2":"Improving an academic draft standardly subsumes checking that the problem–solution argument is internally coherent; the logical arc from free-form-message failures to schema-validated handoffs is the paper's spine and a natural revision target.","R1.6":"The most direct and minimal reading of 'Improve this draft' is sentence- and paragraph-level writing polish, making this the clear load-bearing dimension regardless of scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvements be limited to writing clarity and sentence-level polish, or should they also include substantive revisions to the argument structure, contribution framing, and claim-evidence alignment (e.g., the unsupported 23% figure and the 'first framework' novelty claim)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The draft is built around a gap statement and a strong novelty positioning claim ('first framework to enforce typed inter-agent communication'); improving the draft plausibly requires sharpening both even without an explicit instruction to do so.
rationale.R1.2: Improving an academic draft standardly subsumes checking that the problem–solution argument is internally coherent; the logical arc from free-form-message failures to schema-validated handoffs is the paper's spine and a natural revision target.
rationale.R1.6: The most direct and minimal reading of 'Improve this draft' is sentence- and paragraph-level writing polish, making this the clear load-bearing dimension regardless of scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements be limited to writing clarity and sentence-level polish, or should they also include substantive revisions to the argument structure, contribution framing, and claim-evidence alignment (e.g., the unsupported 23% figure and the 'first framework' novelty claim)?
